source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A piston held by a pin in an insulated cylinder contains 2 kg of water at 100\circ\text{C}, with a quality of 98%. The piston has a mass of 102 kg, with cross-sectional area of 100 cm^2, and the ambient pressure is 100 kPa. The pin is released, which allows the piston to move. Determine the final temperature of the water, assuming the process to be adiabatic.

The piston has a mass of 102 kg, with cross-sectional area of 100 cm^2, and the ambient pressure is 100 kPa. The pin is released, which allows the piston to move. Determine the final temperature of the water, assuming the process to be adiabatic.
